description: Build the complete NBA Draft prediction application: project structure, data models, web scrapers for multiple mock draft sources, weighted consensus aggregation engine, backtest validation against 2024 draft, and CLI entry point that outputs a 2026 draft board.
steps: Create project skeleton: requirements.txt (requests, beautifulsoup4, pytest, lxml), main.py, config.py, models.py, aggregator.py, backtest.py, scrapers/__init__.py, data/, output/, tests/
Implement config.py: source weights (ESPN=0.9, Tankathon=0.85, NBADraft.net=0.8, betting_odds=0.95), time decay factor, output settings
Implement models.py: PlayerPrediction dataclass (name, position, school/team, projected_pick, source, date, confidence), DraftBoard class with ranked list
Implement scrapers/nbadraft_net.py: scrape mock draft data from nbadraft.net using requests + BeautifulSoup. Parse player name, pick number, school. Handle connection errors gracefully.
Implement scrapers/tankathon.py: scrape tankathon.com mock draft. Parse player name, pick number, team, school.
Implement scrapers/espn.py: scrape ESPN mock draft page. Handle ESPN's structure to extract player rankings.
Implement scrapers/odds.py: scrape NBA draft betting odds from available odds sites (e.g. sportsbettingdime, covers.com). Parse implied probability from odds.
Implement scrapers/__init__.py: unified interface that runs all scrapers, handles failures gracefully (if one source fails, continue with others), returns list of PlayerPrediction objects

Implement aggregator.py: WeightedConsensusEngine that takes predictions from multiple sources, normalizes player names (fuzzy matching for slight variations), applies source weight * time_decay * confidence, calculates consensus pick position for each player, produces final ranked DraftBoard
Implement data/actual_2024_draft.json: historical data of actual 2024 NBA Draft results (top 30 picks) for backtesting

Implement backtest.py: load 2024 mock draft data (hardcoded historical predictions from known sources at that time), run through aggregation engine, compare against actual_2024_draft.json, calculate accuracy metrics (exact match %, within-3-picks %, Kendall tau correlation)

Implement main.py: CLI with argparse. --mode predict (default): run scrapers, aggregate, output 2026 prediction. --mode backtest: run 2024 validation. Print readable draft board to terminal with pick#, player name, position, school, consensus score. Save JSON to output/
Create tests/test_aggregator.py: test weighted consensus with known inputs, test name normalization, test time decay
Create tests/test_backtest.py: test that backtest runs and produces accuracy metrics
Create tests/test_models.py: test data model creation and serialization
Test the full application: run pytest, run main.py --mode backtest to verify it works end-to-end, run main.py --mode predict to verify scraping works (handle gracefully if some sites block)
acceptance_criteria: python -m pytest tests/ -v passes all tests
python main.py --mode backtest runs successfully and prints accuracy metrics for 2024 draft
python main.py --mode predict runs successfully, scrapes at least 1 source, and outputs a 30-pick draft board
Output JSON files are written to output/ directory
Terminal output shows a readable formatted draft board
Graceful error handling when a scraping source is unavailable
findings: All 29 tests pass. Backtest shows 33.3% exact match, 76.7% within-3-picks, 0.8529 Kendall tau. Predict mode successfully scrapes 3/4 sources (ESPN requires auth/paywall). Fallback data is available if all scrapers fail. Unicode name normalization handles accent variations.
